note: 58M presenting with acute crushing chest pain radiating to left arm. Diaphoretic, pale. BP 180/110, HR 122, RR 24. EKG shows ST elevation in anterior leads. Onset 2 hours ago.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Acute ST elevation MI with acute coronary syndrome criteria. Immediate catheterization indicated.

note: 72F rushed in with sudden loss of consciousness. Last known well 45 minutes ago. Wife reports facial drooping on right side. Left arm weak. Speech slurred. BP 210/125. Glucose 125. Time critical.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Acute ischemic stroke within 1 hour of symptom onset. Immediate CT and tPA evaluation needed.

note: 32F with severe difficulty breathing. Throat feels tight. Urticaria on face and trunk. Exposure to shellfish 20 minutes ago. Wheezing audible. O2 sat 88% on RA.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Acute anaphylaxis with respiratory compromise. Immediate epinephrine IM required.

note: 45M with fever 103.9F, altered mental status, neck stiffness, severe headache. Brought in by ambulance. BP 92/58. HR 118. Petechial rash on extremities. WBC likely elevated.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Septic shock with presumed meningitis. Requires blood cultures, antibiotics, ICU monitoring.

note: 29M with penetrating stab wound to left lower abdomen. Unconscious. BP 76/52, HR 142, RR 26. Pale and clammy. Massive ongoing bleeding. ETA trauma requires ongoing transfusion.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Class 3 hemorrhagic shock from penetrating trauma. Requires immediate OR.

note: 51M presenting with sudden severe headache described as 'worst of my life'. Neck stiffness. Photophobia. Vomiting. Alert at 15. No known trauma. BP 165/95.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Classic subarachnoid hemorrhage presentation. Requires immediate CT and neurosurgery consult.

note: 67F with acute shortness of breath, pleuritic chest pain, hypoxia. Unilateral leg swelling with calf pain. Recent long flight. BP 135/82, HR 105, O2 98% on 2L NC.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: High-risk PE presentation with DVT. Requires CT angiography and anticoagulation.

note: 38M trauma: motorcycle vs truck at 50 mph. GCS 14, pupils reactive. Compound fracture left tibia with heavy bleeding. Abdominal exam with bruising. BP 120/78, HR 94, RR 20.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Significant blunt trauma requiring FAST exam, imaging, orthopedic consult.

note: 84F with uncontrolled seizure activity, presented seizing. Postictal but now has focal neurological deficits. Known epileptic with recent med non-compliance. On board O2. BP 148/88, HR 82.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Active seizure disorder with compliance issues. Requires AED levels, EEG, ICU monitoring.

note: 41M brought in by paramedics, unresponsive, pupils pinpoint. Empty opioid bottle found at scene. Respiratory depression, RR 8. O2 sat 82%. Requires immediate naloxone reversal.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Acute opioid overdose with respiratory failure. Needs naloxone, intubation standby.

note: 28F with acute fever 39.5C, joint pain (especially hands), butterfly rash on cheeks, photosensitivity. Also has mild shortness of breath. Known lupus family history. ESR elevated on recent bloodwork.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Lupus flare vs acute infection. Requires ANA, complement levels, blood cultures to differentiate.

note: 19F college student with acute dyspnea, chest pain pleuritic type, anxiety-like symptoms. Recent upper respiratory infection. D-dimer elevated. BP 118/76, HR 112, RR 24, O2 95%.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: PE cannot be excluded despite anxiety presentation. CT angiography required. D-dimer positive is red flag.

note: 31F with severe abdominal pain lower quadrant, nausea, dizziness. Missed period 6 weeks. Vaginal spotting present. BP 105/72, HR 95. Quantitative hCG pending. Ectopic ruled out?

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Lower abdominal pain + missed period + spotting = ectopic pregnancy until proven otherwise. Requires ultrasound STAT.

note: 23M with fruity breath odor, severe abdominal pain, vomiting, altered mental status. Brought by roommate. Glucose 523 on glucose meter. Respirations deep and rapid. Recently started college.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Diabetic ketoacidosis vs acute intoxication (fruity breath). Requires immediate labs, insulin, fluids, ICU monitoring.

note: 45M patient with fever, headache, neck stiffness, altered mental status. Wife says he was acting confused. Petechial rash on trunk. Fever 38.9C. Also recent travel to endemic region.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Meningitis vs viral encephalitis vs dengue/travel-related illness. Requires LP, broad antibiotics, travel history details.

note: 52F with sudden severe unilateral headache, vision changes, jaw claudication when eating. Tender temporal artery. ESR elevated. Age 52. Polymyalgia rheumatica symptoms present.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Giant cell arteritis vs migraine. ESR elevation + age + symptoms = high risk for vision-threatening disease.

note: 34F with palpitations, anxiety, tremor, heat intolerance. Warm skin, sweaty. Resting HR 118. Labs show suppressed TSH. Weight loss 15 lbs in 2 months despite good appetite.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Thyroid storm vs anxiety. Suppressed TSH + physical findings = endocrine emergency. Requires PTU/propranolol.

note: 48M with severe leg pain, redness, warmth. Recent long drive. Unilateral calf swelling. Temperature 38.5C. Mild dyspnea. BP 128/85, HR 102, RR 20. Recent surgery 2 weeks ago.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: DVT vs cellulitis vs compartment syndrome. Post-op status + unilateral swelling = DVT high probability. Ultrasound needed.

note: 42M with severe headache, fever 103.2C, neck stiffness, confusion. Recently treated for ear infection 2 weeks ago. Now has stiff neck and light sensitivity. GCS 14.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Post-otitis meningitis. Recent ear infection history is key. Requires immediate LP, cultures, antibiotics.

note: 55M with sudden severe headache, neck pain, sweating, agitation. BP 188/115. HR 102. Recent cocaine use admitted by patient. Narcotics given. Alert and oriented.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Cocaine-induced hypertensive emergency vs intracranial hemorrhage. Requires CT head, cardiology consult, careful BP management.

note: 67F with chest pain, SOB, EKG changes. Likely NSTEMI. Needs cardiac monitoring.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: NSTEMI presentation requiring cardiac cath.

note: 29M pedestrian struck by vehicle. GCS 13, left femur fracture with bleeding. Unstable pelvic exam. Trauma activation.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Class 2 hemorrhagic shock, needs OR immediately.

note: 81M with fever 38.8C, confusion, urinary retention. Recent UTI history. BP 105/62, HR 98. Sepsis screening positive.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Sepsis from UTI source, requires fluids and antibiotics.

note: 44F presenting with acute severe headache, photophobia, neck stiffness. Temperature 39.2C. Recent sick contact. Petechiae on legs.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Bacterial meningitis, needs antibiotics stat and ICU.

note: 56M with acute abdominal pain, vomiting, distended abdomen. BP 120/75, HR 105, abdomen rigid on exam. Recent surgery 3 days ago.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Likely post-op bowel obstruction or perforation.

note: 73F with severe shortness of breath, orthopnea, leg edema. Recent MI 6 months ago. Crackles on exam. O2 88%. Requires intubation readiness.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Cardiogenic shock, needs immediate diuretics and possible intubation.

note: 35M with severe facial trauma from assault. Facial fractures, airway compromise risk. GCS 14. Profuse bleeding from nose and mouth.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Facial trauma with airway compromise, needs ENT and oral surgery standby.

note: 62M with sudden weakness left side, slurred speech. Arrived 30 min ago. NIHSS 12. Glucose normal. Candidate for thrombolytics.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Acute CVA within treatment window, needs CT and tPA consideration.

note: 28F with diffuse rash, fever 39.8C, headache, muscle aches. Rapid onset over 6 hours. Looks toxic. Possible meningococcemia.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Meningococcemia with septic shock, immediate antibiotics and ICU.

note: 52F with sudden vision loss right eye, floaters, flashing lights. Concern for retinal detachment. Able to walk and communicate clearly.

ground_truth.clinician_reasoning: Retinal detachment requires urgent but not emergent op evaluation.
